channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Premier League great Salah will leave lifetime of memories
item.description: Mohamed Salah will leave Liverpool as an all-time great and iconic Premier League figure, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Salah to leave Liverpool at end of season
item.description: The Egypt forward says Liverpool fans "gave me the best time of my life" and "stood by me in the toughest times".

item.title: How Scotland reached the World Cup - told by those who made it happen
item.description: Tom English speaks to those who returned Scotland to the men's World Cup finals for the first time since 1998 about the epic win over Denmark.

item.title: Keep Tudor? Go for De Zerbi, Mason or Redknapp? Where do Spurs go next?
item.description: BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty asks what Spurs should do next with uncertainty surrounding the future of interim head coach Igor Tudor.

item.title: Man Utd retain new stadium dream for 2035 Women's World Cup
item.description: Manchester United are still aiming to have their new stadium built and ready to host the 2035 Women's World Cup final.

item.title: Does running more in a game actually make a difference?
item.description: Tottenham significantly outran Nottingham Forest on Sunday but still ended up losing 3-0, so does running more make a difference?

item.title: Arsenal's Eze could be out for month with injury
item.description: Arsenal attacking midfielder Eberechi Eze could miss between four to six weeks because of a calf injury.

item.title: 'What a stunner!' - Kelly doubles Arsenal's lead against Chelsea
item.description: Chloe Kelly's curling long-range strike puts Arsenal 2-0 up in the first leg of their Women's Champions League quarter-final against Chelsea.

item.title: Salah's best goals for Liverpool
item.description: Watch some of the standout goals scored by Mohamed Salah for Liverpool. The Egyptian forward has revealed he will leave the club at the end of the season.

item.title: Salah's five-hour bus journey to football training aged 12
item.description: How Mohamed Salah defied the odds and never gave up on his challenging journey from rural Egypt to become a footballer.

item.title: Arsenal's Smith v Chelsea's Thompson in Mastermind quiz
item.description: Chelsea's Alyssa Thompson and Arsenal's Olivia Smith go head-to-head in a Mastermind quiz, choosing Los Angeles and Harry Potter as their specialist subjects.

item.title: Should Raya have started over Arrizabalaga in final?
item.description: The Monday Night Club's Rory Smith and Chris Sutton debate whether Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta made the correct decision in starting Kepa Arrizabalaga over David Raya in the Carabao Cup final.

item.title: I miss English football - Pochettino on 'ideal place' to manage
item.description: Former Tottenham and Chelsea manager Mauricio Pochettino says he misses "the world of football in England".

item.title: Football's great entertainers - ranking the biggest showboaters
item.description: We all love to watch the players with the flicks and tricks, the mavericks with the magic manoeuvres. Here, BBC Sport ranks football's top showboaters.

item.title: PSG request to move Lens match before Liverpool Champions League tie
item.description: PSG ask to postpone their Ligue 1 game against title rivals Lens which is sandwiched between the two legs of their Champions League quarter-final against Liverpool.

item.title: Griezmann to join MLS side Orlando from Atletico
item.description: Former France forward Antoine Griezmann will join MLS side Orlando City from Atletico Madrid this summer.

item.title: Is the three-week Premier League break good for your side?
item.description: The Premier League is on a break for almost three weeks as international football takes centre stage, but will it give your club a chance to reset or disrupt momentum?

item.title: Who can still qualify for the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which countries can still qualify for the World Cup via the European play-offs and intercontinental play-offs.

item.title: Complaint filed over World Cup ticket prices
item.description: Football Supporters Europe files a formal complaint against Fifa over "excessive ticket prices" and "unfair purchasing conditions" for the World Cup.

item.title: Sexism in football - a problem that isn't going away
item.description: BBC Sport speaks to fans, the police and others to examine how common sexism is in football, why it happens, and what can be done.

item.title: 'Northern Ireland have nothing to fear but everything to gain'
item.description: In a column for BBC Sport NI, Stuart Dallas says Northern Ireland "have nothing to fear but everything to gain" in their World Cup play-off in Italy.

item.title: Williams aims to step out of shadows of Welsh icons
item.description: As Wales look to secure qualification for the World Cup, Neco Williams describes how following in the footsteps of Welsh icons can be an inspiration and a burden.

item.title: Dirt pitches, dodging dogs and choosing Chelsea - the making of Estevao
item.description: From the dirt pitch to practising against a rottweiler and Thiago Silva's advice - the foundations of Chelsea forward Estevao's rise.

item.title: EFL play-off finals on Spring Bank Holiday weekend
item.description: This season's English Football League play-off finals at Wembley will take place over the Spring Bank Holiday weekend in May.

item.title: Tuchel clearly doesn't rate Alexander-Arnold - Sutton
item.description: The Monday Night Club's Chris Sutton and Rory Smith discuss Thomas Tuchel's decision to exclude Trent Alexander-Arnold from the latest England squad.

item.title: 'Capitulation' - how 'fragile' Tottenham put Tudor under pressure
item.description: Kelly Cates, Joe Hart and Danny Murphy discuss how a "fragile" Tottenham "fell apart" against Nottingham Forest, and what this could mean for interim boss Igor Tudor's position.

item.title: Have Newcastle gone backwards?
item.description: Match of the Day's Kelly Cates, Joe Hart and Danny Murphy discuss how Newcastle are "struggling with consistency" since competing in the Champions League.

item.title: How 'confident' McGinn makes the difference for Villa
item.description: Kelly Cates, Joe Hart, Danny Murphy discuss the impact of midfielder John McGinn's "confidence" and "creativity" for Aston Villa in his side's 2-0 victory against West Ham.

item.title: How 'all-action' Anderson inspired Forest win
item.description: Kelly Cates, Joe Hart and Danny Murphy discuss why Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson is "the favourite" to be in the starting XI for England in this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Cooper gets apology in Wales v Bosnia dropping row
item.description: Brondby's Welsh boss Steve Cooper has been given an apology after being accused of dropping a Bosnia player before the World Cup play-off against Wales.

item.title: The player who tackled cancer - and now wants to take on the world
item.description: Forced to be a fan at Qatar 2022 by a life-threatening illness, Wales midfielder David Brooks is determined to play at the 2026 World Cup finals.

item.title: Is Scotland cap next step on Stewart's 'surreal' journey?
item.description: Barney Stewart was not even a professional footballer 15 months ago. Now he is representing his country and harbours Scottish Cup ambitions with Falkirk.

item.title: Volcanoes, connection & disrespect - the making of Hallgrimsson
item.description: From a small volcanic island to becoming a national hero, Heimir Hallgrimsson's journey to Republic of Ireland manager is an unlikely success story.

item.title: Sancho in talks with Dortmund - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: England winger Jadon Sancho might return to Borussia Dortmund, Zinedine Zidane agrees to become France manager and Roberto de Zerbi lines up the Tottenham job.

item.title: Carter, Eze and Iwobi named on Football Black List
item.description: England defender Jess Carter, Arsenal attacker Eberechi Eze and Fulham midfielder Alex Iwobi are on the 2025 Football Black List.

item.title: Wilson's Fulham form 'no surprise to Wales' - Ampadu
item.description: Ethan Ampadu says Harry Wilson’s outstanding Fulham form comes as no surprise to those inside the Wales camp.

item.title: 'Man City would be stupid to let Shaw go!'
item.description: BBC Sport pundits Fara Williams and Nia Jones explain why Bunny Shaw is the best forward in the game right now.

item.title: Forest win sinks Tottenham closer to relegation danger
item.description: Spurs remain winless in the league since the turn of the year, as Nottingham Forest sail to a comfortable 3-0 victory at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.

item.title: Late Brobbey winner helps Sunderland beat Newcastle
item.description: Brian Brobbey scores in the 90th minute for Sunderland as the Black Cats come from behind to beat rivals Newcastle United 2-1 in the Tyne-Wear derby at St James' Park.

item.title: Watkins ends goal drought as Villa beat West Ham
item.description: Ollie Watkins ends his six-game goal drought, as Aston Villa win 2-0 over relegation-threatened West Ham United.

item.title: O'Shea hopes for 'straightforward' contract talks
item.description: Republic of Ireland coach John O'Shea says he hopes negotiations on a contract extension will be "straightforward" but "all focus is on the game ahead".

item.title: Newcastle v the system - is Saudi 2030 vision unrealistic?
item.description: Newcastle want to be one of the top clubs in the world by 2030. BBC Sport examines why their Saudi owners are going to have to show a lot more ambition to get anywhere near that.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and, it's about to become a bit more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Real talk is nonsense but I may coach again - Klopp
item.description: Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp says speculation he will return to management with Real Madrid this summer is "nonsense".

item.title: Foden final appearance felt like a 'charity' substitution - Rooney
item.description: Phil Foden's injury-time appearance in Manchester City's Carabao Cup final victory over Arsenal felt like a "charity" substitution, says former England captain Wayne Rooney.

item.title: Celtic 'manufactured' title race and need five or six wins - McGregor
item.description: Captain Callum McGregor has no doubt Celtic remain in the Scottish title race but says they need "at least five or six" wins from their final seven matches.

item.title: Taking wildcards, leaving out stars - the art of World Cup squad building
item.description: Managers often get it wrong when it comes to picking World Cup squads - is one packed with stars always the best option?

item.title: 'Everyone knows Spurs are in a relegation battle'
item.description: Coach Bruno Saltor admits everyone at Tottenham is aware they are in a relegation battle, and says a 3-0 Premier League defeat at home to fellow strugglers Nottingham Forest was a "tough one to take".

item.title: Leicester relegation fears grow as Villa battle back to win
item.description: Leicester City remain at the bottom of the WSL table after Aston Villa mount a come back from one goal down to win 2-1 at King Power Stadium.

item.title: Trafford 'sets the tone', while Kepa suffers 'a bad moment'
item.description: Ex-Manchester City keeper Joe Hart looks at the contrasting Carabao Cup final performances of goalkeepers James Trafford and Kepa Arrizabalaga for Man City and Arsenal, respectively

item.title: 'He's a fighter' - Emery backs Watkins after England omission
item.description: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery has high praise for goalscorer Ollie Watkins, who was left out of Thomas Tuchel's England squad this week, as they beat West Ham 2-0.

item.title: Pain of losing derby to Sunderland 'very real' - Howe
item.description: Newcastle United manager Eddie Howe says his players understand the pain the fans feel after they lost their second Tyne-Wear derby of the season following a late defeat to Sunderland.

item.title: Unfocused West Ham were not good enough - Nuno
item.description: West Ham United manager Nuno Espirito Santo admits Aston Villa were the better team, while his side were unable to find their focus and get comfortable as they lost 2-0 at Villa Park.

item.title: Four hours inside Bellamy's office - and the Wales boss' complex mind
item.description: Wales boss Craig Bellamy discusses everything from football to Balkan history - and playing Ken and Barbie - in an exclusive, in-depth interview with BBC Sport.

item.title: Will 'risky' Gattuso lead Italy back to World Cup?
item.description: Is Gennaro Gattuso ready to guide Italy, who have not qualified for a World Cup since 2014, to the finals in the USA, Canada and Mexico this summer?

item.title: Have Chelsea progressed under Rosenior? Analysing his Blues tenure
item.description: Liam Rosenior is facing criticism early in his tenure as Chelsea manager - but is it fair, and are his players on board as they head for the climax to the season?

item.title: Guardiola's dance of joy as Man City pile pressure on desolate Arsenal
item.description: Manchester City will hope their dominant Carabao Cup final win piles pressure on Arsenal in the Premier League title race, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Six touches in the box, six goals - the epitome of clinical?
item.description: Is QPR's 6-1 rout of Portsmouth - in which they only had six touches in the opposition's penalty area - the most clinical performance ever?

item.title: The race no-one wants to win - are Everton serious top-five contenders?
item.description: The fight for Champions League football appears to be turning into one no-one wants to win, but have Everton snuck into the race?

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: What happens when a manager gets suspended?
item.description: Ever wondered what a touchline ban entails? BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain what punishment managers actually face.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: Nawrocki permanent exit mooted as Just & Ashia linked with Glasgow moves - gossip
item.description: Celtic defender could leave permanently as two players are linked with moves to Glasgow.

item.title: 'If it's right for me, I'd love to stay' - Moore revels in Rangers loan
item.description: Mikey Moore insists he has "loved every single second" of his loan spell at Rangers, with the Tottenham Hotspur forward open to a return next season "if it's right" for the teenager.

item.title: Why ex-Palace winger Olise is now in Ballon d'Or contention at Bayern
item.description: Michael Olise has been in superb form for Bayern Munich this season, has the French winger put himself in contention for the Ballon d'Or?

item.title: Laporta re-elected Barcelona president for fourth term
item.description: Joan Laporta vows Barcelona will be "unstoppable" as he is re-elected club president for a further five years.

item.title: From Bodo to Brentford - ranking this season's biggest overachievers
item.description: Bodo/Glimt must be the most romantic story in European football in decades but are they the team that has defied the odds the most this season? Here are all 10 contenders...

item.title: Cole appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena
item.description: Former Arsenal and Chelsea defender Ashley Cole appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena until the end of the season.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
